data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`), but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `@(Compile)` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `@(Compile)` item group. The MSBuild compilation picks up `Compile` items before the target runs, so even though the file is written to disk, it's not included in compilation.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group via `<ItemGroup>` inside the target:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />


</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added after the file is written.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild's compilation step only compiles files in that item group, so the generated file was silently ignored.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` item inside the target (after the file is written) so the compiler picks it up. An `<ItemGroup>` inside a `<Target>` is evaluated at execution time, which is exactly what's needed for dynamically generated sources.
